perturbation.original_text: Heritage shall within [***] after the end of the production run, notify Premier via email of the final estimated production quantity and the estimated quantity, including losses, of all Premier-supplied materials used. If the final production quantity for any accepted PO is less than [***] of the PO quantity ordered, or if the quantity of production released for shipment within [***] from the last day of production is less than [***] of the PO quantity, upon request by Premier, Heritage shall take all commercially reasonable steps to produce or replace the shortfall within [***]. The final production quantity by Heritage and Jasper will count towards the MAOV requirements.
perturbation.changed_text: Heritage shall within [***] after the end of the production run, notify Premier via email of the final estimated production quantity and the estimated quantity, including losses, of all Premier-supplied materials used. If the final production quantity for any accepted PO is less than [***] of the PO quantity ordered, or if the quantity of production released for shipment within [***] from the last day of production is less than [***] of the PO quantity, upon request by Premier, Heritage may take steps to address any shortfall but is not obligated to produce or replace it. The final production quantity by Heritage and Jasper will count towards the MAOV requirements.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation removes the obligation for Heritage to 'take all commercially reasonable steps to produce or replace the shortfall' and replaces it with 'may take steps to address any shortfall but is not obligated to produce or replace it'. This introduces an in-text contradiction with Section 2(o)(i), which states that 'All Products manufactured, packaged and delivered by Heritage or Jasper under the terms of this Agreement shall conform to the specifications supplied to Heritage by Premier'. Now, there is ambiguity as to whether Heritage is obligated to meet the specifications if the production run results in a shortfall, creating uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Heritage represents and warrants that: (i) All Products manufactured, packaged and delivered by Heritage or Jasper under the terms of this Agreement shall conform to the specifications supplied to Heritage by Premier as listed on Schedule A, which Schedule may from time to time be modified by Premier in writing (the "Specifications"), shall conform to Post Holdings' Quality Expectations Manual attached hereto as Schedule E, and shall conform in all material respects to samples previously supplied to Premier by Heritage. No change in Specifications shall be binding on Heritage until Premier has provided written Specifications for each SKU, and each Specification is signed and dated by the Parties. Any additional net cost increases or decreases associated with any modifications to Premier's Specifications shall be borne by or credited to Premier.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the obligation for Heritage to 'take all commercially reasonable steps to produce or replace the shortfall' and replaces it with 'may take steps to address any shortfall but is not obligated to produce or replace it'. This directly contradicts the warranty in the Contradicted Text that 'All Products manufactured, packaged and delivered by Heritage or Jasper under the terms of this Agreement shall conform to the specifications supplied to Heritage by Premier'. By removing the obligation to address shortfalls, the changed text introduces ambiguity and a direct conflict with the requirement to conform to specifications, as Heritage is no longer explicitly required to meet those specifications if a shortfall occurs. This qualifies as an in-text contradiction as described in the prompt. The change makes the requirement to conform to specifications unenforceable in cases of a shortfall.

perturbation.original_text: Heritage shall perform, and shall ensure Jasper performs, a documented inspection of all trailers before loading to confirm they are free of any visible contamination or odors and fit for use with food products. When products are properly palletized and loaded by Heritage or Jasper, Premier shall be responsible for physical, in-transit damage loss of finished Products upon Heritage or Jasper completing loading of the designated container or trailer, and sealing the same.
perturbation.changed_text: Heritage shall perform, and shall ensure Jasper performs, a documented inspection of all trailers before loading to confirm they are seemingly free of visible contamination or odors and appear fit for use with food products. When products are palletized and loaded by Heritage or Jasper, Premier shall be responsible for physical, in-transit damage loss of finished Products upon Heritage or Jasper completing loading of the designated container or trailer, and sealing the same.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation changes 'free of any visible contamination or odors and fit for use with food products' to 'seemingly free of visible contamination or odors and appear fit for use with food products'. This introduces ambiguity by weakening the standard of inspection, creating an in-text contradiction with Section 8(a), which states 'Heritage shall bear the risk of loss to the Products until the Products are delivered to such carrier for delivery to Premier as set forth herein.' If Heritage only 'seemingly' ensures the trailers are free from contamination, there is now uncertainty about who bears the risk if contamination is discovered after loading, potentially leading to legal disputes. The word 'properly' has also been removed from the description of palletization, adding to the ambiguity on whose responsibility lies on.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Title to the Products shall be in and remain with Premier from the date Products are delivered to a carrier pursuant to Premier's instructions for delivery to Premier. Heritage shall bear the risk of loss to the Products until the Products are delivered to such carrier for delivery to Premier as set forth herein. Risk of loss to the Products shall also be with Heritage during shipment between the Heritage Facilities pursuant to Section 2.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'free of any visible contamination' to 'seemingly free of visible contamination' weakens the standard of inspection, creating ambiguity about Heritage's responsibility for contamination discovered after loading. This directly contradicts the original text. This introduces uncertainty about who bears the risk if contamination is discovered after loading, potentially leading to legal disputes. This directly contradicts the original clause, which states 'Heritage shall bear the risk of loss to the Products until the Products are delivered to such carrier for delivery to Premier as set forth herein', because it is no longer definitively clear that contamination is covered under this section.

perturbation.original_text: Heritage agrees to disclose to Premier and provide a list, upon request, of any material violations or deficiencies noted during any inspection by the FDA, United States Department of Agriculture, PHS, or any other federal, state or local health or food regulatory agency of the Heritage Facilities, Jasper Facility or any other Facility used to produce the Products, which have a material adverse effect on the manufacture or packaging of the Products. Heritage agrees to provide to Premier each FDA Form 483 and any related Establishment Inspection Report ("EIR") that is received from the FDA by Heritage or Jasper, along with any response provided to the regulatory authority by Heritage or Jasper, as long as this Agreement is in effect. Heritage agrees to do so within [***] of Heritage or Jasper receiving the Report or of sending the response as appropriate.
perturbation.changed_text: Heritage agrees to disclose to Premier and provide a list, upon request, of any significant violations or deficiencies noted during any inspection by the FDA, United States Department of Agriculture, PHS, or any other federal, state or local health or food regulatory agency of the Heritage Facilities, Jasper Facility or any other Facility used to produce the Products, which Heritage reasonably believes have a material adverse effect on the manufacture or packaging of the Products. Heritage agrees to provide to Premier each FDA Form 483 and any related Establishment Inspection Report ("EIR") that is received from the FDA by Heritage or Jasper, along with any response provided to the regulatory authority by Heritage or Jasper, as long as this Agreement is in effect. Heritage will endeavor to do so within [***] of Heritage or Jasper receiving the Report or of sending the response as appropriate.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'material violations' to 'significant violations' and adding 'which Heritage reasonably believes have a material adverse effect' introduces subjectivity into what Heritage is obligated to disclose, there now exists an internal contradiction, because now instead of 'agrees to do so within [***]' it now says 'Heritage will endeavor to do so within [***]'. The term 'significant violations' is ambiguous compared to 'material violations', creating uncertainty about the scope of disclosure. This pertubation impacts the original text in section 6(a)(iv) where 'Heritage shall evaluate Products on a regular schedule at a sufficient frequency to confirm that Products meet the Specifications, including the Post Holdings Quality Expectations Manual' because now whether if a significant violation will be disclosed or not, now depends on Heritage's discretion and it opens a debate on the confirmation that Products meet the Specifications.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Heritage agrees to perform, at its expense, sampling and testing procedures for the Products in accordance with Schedule B, attached hereto, and all applicable governmental regulations. If additional testing, not identified in Schedule B, is required by Premier, a reasonable additional fee will be agreed upon between Heritage and Premier to cover the associated incremental cost. Other quality control items to be performed under this Agreement are as follows: (i) Normal production runs shall require Premier to provide at least two (2) non-work hour phone numbers for Premier employees who can be contacted in the event a problem occurs during a production run not being conducted during normal business hours. Said contacts and contact information shall be listed in Schedule D. (ii) Heritage shall keep retention samples in accordance with Schedule B. (iii) Heritage shall not modify any processing instructions or Specifications without obtaining Premier's prior written consent. (iv) Heritage shall evaluate Products on a regular schedule at a sufficient frequency to confirm that Products meet the Specifications, including the Post Holdings Quality Expectations Manual. Any Products not conforming to the Specifications shall not be released for shipment.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a level of subjectivity ('Heritage reasonably believes') and reduces the obligation's stringency ('will endeavor to do so') compared to the original requirement to disclose 'material violations' and 'agrees to do so within [***]'. This directly contradicts the requirement in the 'Contradicted Text' that 'Heritage shall evaluate Products on a regular schedule at a sufficient frequency to confirm that Products meet the Specifications'. The subjectivity and reduced obligation now allows Heritage to potentially not disclose violations it does not deem 'significant' or does not 'reasonably believe' have a material adverse effect, undermining the ability to confirm Products meet Specifications.
